headline: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos class model, dominates this cycle across the recommended educators.
body: Multiple recommended channels converge on Claude Fable 5: Better Stack reports it tops SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code with extended context and vision [10], IBM Technology covers its tier routing system [1], Cyril Imhof details its guardrails, memory, and agentic payment integration versus Mythos 5 [2], and Cole Medin live tests whether it justifies a 2x price premium over Claude Opus 4.8 [3]. Agent tooling is the second thread: Google's open-source Agents CLI ships sandboxed execution, audit trails, and identity management [7], CopilotKit renders real UI and human-in-the-loop workflows in SaaS apps [0], and PAI builds persistent memory and structured workflows on Claude Code [8]. On infrastructure, Groq's Jonathan Ross discusses LPU inference economics and an NVIDIA agreement [4], IBM notes Apple's WWDC 2026 shift from on-device AI to NVIDIA cloud compute [1], and NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 uses hybrid mamba transformer and Latent MoE architectures [6]. Security coverage spans AI-detected zero-days [5] and a self-replicating AI worm with nonhuman identity breaches [9].
